content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Remediated both pending amendments. Final-review planned-elsewhere canonicalization now builds short-alias targets only from canonical bead IDs that are actually allowed to provide aliases, preserving the Nearby work same-milestone safety rule so foreign nearby beads cannot make a local short alias ambiguous. Prompt-review Nearby work body extraction now normalizes heading lines with trailing whitespace the same way the canonical prompt validator does, so valid contract-marked prompts with headings like `## Nearby work ` still receive verbatim Nearby work drift protection. Added focused regression tests for both cases.

## Steps

0. [Completed] Inspected the affected final-review canonicalizer, prompt contract ID extractors, prompt-review Nearby work drift guard, and current local diff.
1. [Completed] Added prompt-contract extraction for canonical bead IDs that may provide short-form aliases, preserving broad aliases for Already Planned Elsewhere while restricting Nearby work aliases to the active milestone.
2. [Completed] Updated `PlannedElsewhereIdCanonicalizer` to build ambiguous short-alias tracking from the safe alias-target set instead of every canonical nearby ID.
3. [Completed] Updated prompt-review canonical section extraction to compare trimmed heading lines, matching the validator's trailing-whitespace tolerance.
4. [Completed] Added regression coverage for foreign nearby IDs not poisoning a local safe alias and for rewritten Nearby work being detected when the original heading has trailing space.
5. [Completed] Ran focused and full repository validation gates, including the authoritative Nix build.

## Validation Evidence

- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub foreign_nearby_work_id_does_not_poison_safe_local_short_alias --lib` passed.
- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub canonical_contract_drift_flags_rewritten_nearby_work_with_trailing_heading_space --lib` passed.
- `nix develop -c cargo fmt --check` passed.
- `nix develop -c cargo clippy --locked -- -D warnings` passed.
- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub` passed.
- `nix build` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.

## Outstanding Risks

None identified.
